speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How fares my lord? Speak, Beaufort, to thy sovereign.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: If thou be’st Death, I’ll give thee England’s treasure,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Enough to purchase such another island,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: So thou wilt let me live and feel no pain.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, what a sign it is of evil life,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where Death’s approach is seen so terrible!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Beaufort, it is thy sovereign speaks to thee.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Bring me unto my trial when you will.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Died he not in his bed? Where should he die?

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Can I make men live, whe’er they will or no?

speaker: CARDINAL
text: O, torture me no more! I will confess.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Alive again? Then show me where he is.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: I’ll give a thousand pound to look upon him.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: He hath no eyes! The dust hath blinded them.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Comb down his hair. Look, look. It stands upright,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Like lime-twigs set to catch my wingèd soul.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Give me some drink, and bid the apothecary

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Bring the strong poison that I bought of him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, Thou eternal mover of the heavens,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Look with a gentle eye upon this wretch!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, beat away the busy meddling fiend

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That lays strong siege unto this wretch’s soul,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And from his bosom purge this black despair!

speaker: WARWICK
text: See how the pangs of death do make him grin!

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Disturb him not. Let him pass peaceably.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Peace to his soul, if God’s good pleasure be!—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lord Card’nal, if thou think’st on heaven’s bliss,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hold up thy hand; make signal of thy hope.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He dies and makes no sign. O, God forgive him!

speaker: WARWICK
text: So bad a death argues a monstrous life.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Forbear to judge, for we are sinners all.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Close up his eyes, and draw the curtain close,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And let us all to meditation.
